# CanPay Insights

> Free Canadian payroll & take-home pay calculator. Computes exact net pay by province and territory for 2026 — federal tax, provincial tax, CPP/CPP2, EI, and Quebec's QPP/QPIP — from a transparent rules engine. Available in English, French, and Chinese, no signup, no paywall.

CanPay Insights is an independent project based in Vancouver, Canada. All figures come from an open calculation engine that applies published Canada Revenue Agency (CRA), Revenu Québec, and provincial/territorial rates. The data is free to cite — please attribute and link to https://canpayinsights.ca.

## Calculators
- [Take-home pay calculator](https://canpayinsights.ca/): net pay by province for hourly wage, annual salary, or timesheet
- [Compare provinces](https://canpayinsights.ca/compare-provinces): same gross salary, net pay side by side across provinces
- [Ontario paycheck calculator](https://canpayinsights.ca/ontario-paycheck-calculator)
- [Quebec paycheck calculator](https://canpayinsights.ca/quebec-paycheck-calculator)
- [CPP & EI calculator](https://canpayinsights.ca/cpp-ei-calculator)
- [Salary after tax in Canada](https://canpayinsights.ca/salary-after-tax-canada)

## Original, citable data articles
- [Minimum-wage take-home pay — all 13 provinces & territories, 2026](https://canpayinsights.ca/blog/minimum-wage-take-home-pay-canada-2026)
- [How much CPP will I pay in 2026? (by salary)](https://canpayinsights.ca/blog/how-much-cpp-will-i-pay-2026)
- [How much EI will I pay in 2026? (by salary)](https://canpayinsights.ca/blog/how-much-ei-will-i-pay-2026)
- [CPP2 (second additional CPP) explained, 2026](https://canpayinsights.ca/blog/cpp2-second-additional-cpp-2026)
- [Alberta vs Ontario taxes, 2025](https://canpayinsights.ca/blog/alberta-vs-ontario-taxes-2025)
- [Guide de la paie au Québec 2026 (FR)](https://canpayinsights.ca/blog/guide-paie-quebec-2026)

## Key 2026 facts
- The federal lowest income-tax bracket is cut from 15% to 14% in 2026.
- CPP: 5.95% on earnings from $3,500 to the YMPE (~$74,600). CPP2: an extra 4% from the YMPE up to the YAMPE (~$85,000).
- EI: 1.63% of insurable earnings, annual maximum ~$1,123 (MIE $68,900). Quebec workers pay a lower EI rate (1.30%, max ~$896) because Quebec runs QPIP separately.
- TFSA contribution limit for 2026: $7,000. RRSP limit: $33,810.
- Example take-home on an $80,000 salary (2026): BC ~$61,038, Ontario ~$60,744, Alberta ~$60,409, Quebec ~$57,077.
- Minimum-wage full-time take-home ranges from ~$26,779/yr (Alberta, $15.00/hr) to ~$34,393/yr (Nunavut, $19.75/hr).
